name: Ukraine (naval ensign)

colors.symbolism: the field of the ensign — purity and the tradition of European naval white ensigns
the Cross of Saint Andrew (Saint George's cross in heraldic blue), patron of seafarers; matches the national flag's blue
the canton — the national flag's yellow

description: A white field charged with a blue Saint George's cross (a couped cross of blue extending to the edges of the flag), with the Ukrainian national flag (blue over yellow) in the canton.
history: The current Ukrainian naval ensign was adopted on 20 June 2006 by presidential decree of Viktor Yushchenko, replacing an earlier post-Soviet design. The white-and-blue Saint George's cross with a national canton consciously echoes the Russian Empire's Saint Andrew's ensign and the broader European naval white-ensign tradition.

features.role: Saint George's cross extending to the edges of the flag
